command.linux: if grep -q '^POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS=' .env; then sed -i.bak 's/^POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS=.*/POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS={timeout}/' .env && rm -f .env.bak; else printf '\nPOLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS={timeout}\n' >> .env; fi
command.windows: powershell -Command "$envFile='.env'; $newLine='POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS={timeout}'; if (Test-Path $envFile) { $content = Get-Content $envFile; if ($content -match '^POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS=') { $content = $content -replace '^POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS=.*', $newLine } else { $content += $newLine }; Set-Content -Path $envFile -Value $content } else { Set-Content -Path $envFile -Value $newLine }"
command.macos: if grep -q '^POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS=' .env; then sed -i.bak 's/^POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS=.*/POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS={timeout}/' .env && rm -f .env.bak; else printf '\nPOLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS={timeout}\n' >> .env; fi

return.description: Whether polling interval was updated successfully
